---
title: "Research projects"
subtitle: "Neology, lexical diffusion, and the study of language change"
description: "An overview of my research programme in neology, corpus linguistics, lexical diffusion, and methodological innovation."
page-layout: article
body-classes: "top-level-page projects-page"
---

::: {.page-heading}
# Research programme statement
:::

My research programme currently comprises four main areas:

1. **Term competition and lexical diffusion**, particularly the factors that influence whether emerging terms become established or remain marginal.
2. **Rare and emerging linguistic phenomena**, including low-frequency constructions that are difficult to identify and study using conventional methods.
3. **Artificial intelligence in linguistic research**, especially the evaluation and responsible use of large language models for corpus analysis, evidence extraction, and research workflows.
4. **Academic writing and scholarly communication**, with particular attention to how disciplinary expectations and genre conventions can be made more explicit for students and researchers.

For individual studies and collaborations, see my [research projects](research-projects.qmd). Published and forthcoming work is listed under [publications](research-publications.qmd).
